title: Source/provenance + citation-coverage enforcement (no source, no claim) + staleness fail-safe
context: Every duty/compliance claim must be backed by a Source record with citation + provenance. A citation-coverage test enforces the no-source-no-claim rule. Each Source carries lastVerified and validUntil (derived from per-source-type review intervals). At runtime a claim past validUntil is auto-flagged or withheld and routed to the re-verification queue; a staleness test asserts no claim serves as current past its window. This makes stale content a visible, gated event rather than a silent error.
objective: Implement the Source/provenance model with citation + provenance fields, enforce the no-source-no-claim rule via a citation-coverage test, and build the staleness fail-safe (lastVerified/validUntil auto-flag/withhold + re-verification queue routing) -- tested on fixtures.
acceptanceCriteria: Source model carries: citation, jurisdiction, URL, retrieval date, legal-status note, lastVerified, validUntil (derived from per-source-type review interval)
Citation-coverage test asserts that no claim surfaces without an attached Source; a missing Source is a test failure

At runtime a claim whose backing Source is past validUntil is auto-flagged (informational surfaces) or withheld (HIGH-severity surfaces) and routed to the re-verification queue; tested with clock-advanced fixtures
A staleness test asserts no claim serves as current past its validUntil window
Re-verification and expert re-sign-off writes a fresh lastVerified/validUntil; a stale source cannot resume normal service without re-sign-off
output: A merged PR with the Source/provenance model, citation-coverage enforcement test, staleness fail-safe (auto-flag/withhold + re-verification queue), and fixture tests for both regimes.
